extraction.one_line_summary: BILBAO Pello won from a 14-rider breakaway after NAILENS Chris was caught with 3km to go despite a 33km solo effort.
tactical_patterns: POGACAR and VINGEGAARD both infiltrated early breakaway within 15km, forcing INEOS to chase despite no GC threat established.
Israel Premier Tech placed two riders (SCHULTZ, NAILENS) in 14-man break, allowing NAILENS 33km solo attack while teammate sat on.
BAUDET Romain and GAUDU David were surprisingly distanced early despite being protected climbers, exposing FDJ/Groupama weakness in heat.

rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: moved to top 5 GC while winning from break, demonstrating dual GC/opportunistic threat capacity
rider_signals.NAILENS Chris: negative: caught with 3km remaining after 33km solo despite teammate support suggests misjudged power reserves
rider_signals.BAUDET Romain: negative: dropped in first 15km on stage suited to his profile indicates poor heat management or form
rider_signals.GAUDU David: negative: also distanced early with Baudet, confirming Groupama-FDJ struggling in extreme heat conditions
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: neutral: animated in break but couldn't win sprint, showing post-Giro fatigue or tactical positioning weakness
